### Post by neon100 on 2009-03-19
I know this is a duplicate but this is getting frustrating. The more i read the DIU texts, the more i get confused. and so far i have not been able to auto-mount BOTH my partitions at login

My fstab file looks like following 

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automatically generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda6 :
UUID=0a16e024-d8cd-4e3b-8971-448887e4db39 / reiserfs notail,relatime 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda7 :
UUID=4fa00d52-3a80-45f1-b4a6-1d3aef7f98d0 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/sda2 /media/kk ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
```

The NTFS configuration tool has helped me automount the NTFS partition only. Whereas the FAT partition is still not getting automounted.

When i type the
```
sudo fdisk -l
```
command, i get the following results.

```

Disk /dev/sda: 60.0 GB, 60011642880 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 7296 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes
Disk identifier: 0x980e980e

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sda1               1           6       48163+   6  FAT16
/dev/sda2   *           7        2591    20764012+   7  HPFS/NTFS
/dev/sda3            2592        7296    37792912+   f  W95 Ext'd (LBA)
/dev/sda5            2592        6512    31495401    b  W95 FAT32
/dev/sda6            6513        7156     5172898+  83  Linux
/dev/sda7            7157        7296     1124518+  82  Linux swap / Solaris

Disk /dev/sdb: 60.0 GB, 60022480896 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 7297 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes
Disk identifier: 0x95aa95aa

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sdb1   *           1        3824    30716248+   c  W95 FAT32 (LBA)
/dev/sdb2            3825        7296    27888840    f  W95 Ext'd (LBA)
/dev/sdb5            3825        7296    27888808+   7  HPFS/NTFS

```

It is this sda5 that i want to auto mount.

---

### Post by taurus on 2009-03-19
You don't have an entry for /dev/sda5 in /etc/fstab so of course, it wouldn't automount.

Edit /etc/fstab

```
gksudo gedit /etc/fstab
```
and add this line to the end of it.

```
/dev/sda5   /media/sda5   vfat   iocharset=utf8,umask=000  0  0
```
Save it and close down gedit.  Then, create that new mount point and mount it.

```
sudo mkdir /media/sda5
sudo mount -a
df -h
```

---

### Post by neon100 on 2009-03-19
It worked. Thanks a zillion.

---
